perturbation.original_text: HPS agrees to outsource to SHPS, and hereby appoints SHPS as the exclusive provider of, Care Management Services to the Clients, subject to the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement. SHPS shall provide the Care Management Services directly to the Clients in accordance with the terms of the Client Agreements. "Care Management Services" means the business of providing utilization review (which includes, but is not limited to, pre-admission certification, prior authorization, prospective length of stay approvals, second opinions, concurrent review and discharge planning), catastrophic medical case management, disease management and demand management (24 hours per day, 7 days per week) services to benefits payors and health providers, in all cases in accordance with the terms of the applicable Client Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: HPS agrees to outsource to SHPS, and hereby appoints SHPS as the exclusive vendor of, Health Assistance to the Clients, subject to the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement. SHPS shall provide the Health Assistance directly to the Clients in accordance with the terms of the Client Agreements. "Health Assistance" means the business of providing limited support to the clients and catastrophic medical case management, disease management and demand management (24 hours per day, 7 days per week) services to benefits payors and health providers, in all cases in accordance with the terms of the applicable Client Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The terms 'Care Management Services' is changed to 'Health Assistance' and 'exclusive provider' to 'exclusive vendor'. This introduces uncertainty because 'Health Assistance' lacks a clear definition and its scope is limited to 'limited support'. This contradicts the later sections that defines Care management service as something more than just 'limited support'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CALCULATION OF CARE MANAGEMENT REVENUES. "Care Management Revenues" means, with respect to any month during the term, the revenues collected by HPS from Clients (or new Clients, as applicable) for the Care Management Services. Monthly revenues for Care Management Services shall be calculated based on a per employee per month fee equal to: (i) the amount (as of the date of this Agreement) set forth in the applicable Client Agreement (including hourly medical case management fees); or (ii) if the Client Agreement does not include a per employee per month fee for Care Management Services, $2.00 (this amount shall apply to all individual and small group business). Prospectively, for new Clients, SHPS and HPS shall agree to the rate HPS will offer to such new Clients (including hourly medical case management fees).
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Care Management Services' with 'Health Assistance' and redefines it to mean 'limited support'. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which defines 'Care Management Revenues' as revenues collected for 'Care Management Services' and includes calculations based on 'hourly medical case management fees'. Since 'Health Assistance' is defined as 'limited support', it is inconsistent with the detailed revenue calculation for 'Care Management Services', implying that the services and associated revenue calculations no longer align, and therefore creating an in-text contradiction. The change makes the calculation of revenue unclear and unenforceable because 'Health Assistance' is not linked to the previous revenue calculation model.

perturbation.original_text: For each month during the term of this Agreement, HPS will pay to SHPS an amount equal to (i) eighty-two and one-half percent (82.5%) of the first $500,000 of Care Management Revenues (as defined below) during such month plus (ii) eighty percent (80%) of Care Management Revenues during such month in excess of $500,000. HPS shall pay such amount to SHPS within fifteen days following the end of the applicable month.
perturbation.changed_text: For each month during the term of this Agreement, HPS will pay to SHPS an amount equal to (i) eighty-two and one-half percent (82.5%) of the first $500,000 of Service Generated Income (as defined below) during such month plus (ii) eighty percent (80%) of Care Management Revenues during such month in excess of $500,000. HPS shall pay such amount to SHPS within fifteen days following the end of the applicable month.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Care Management Revenues' to 'Service Generated Income' for the first $500,000 creates ambiguity. It's unclear if 'Service Generated Income' is equivalent to 'Care Management Revenues' for the purpose of calculation. The term is used interchangably in the same section and this creates an internal contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For each month during the term of this Agreement, HPS will pay to SHPS an amount equal to (i) eighty-two and one-half percent (82.5%) of the first $500,000 of Service Generated Income (as defined below) during such month plus (ii) eighty percent (80%) of Care Management Revenues during such month in excess of $500,000. HPS shall pay such amount to SHPS within fifteen days following the end of the applicable month. At the time of payment HPS shall submit to SHPS a schedule for the month of payment setting forth the calculation of fees payable under this Section 2.1 and Care Management Revenues by Client.
perturbation.justification: The modified text changes 'Care Management Revenues' to 'Service Generated Income' for the first $500,000, while the contradicted text also uses both terms but only defines the calculation based on 'Care Management Revenues by Client'. This creates an ambiguity and potential contradiction because it is unclear if and how 'Service Generated Income' is calculated in relation to 'Care Management Revenues' according to the contract, thus, leading to uncertainty about which terms should be enforced, leading to disputes over contractual obligations, aligning with the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that HPS (or one of its affiliates other than SHPS) enters into an agreement to provide Care Management Services with a client which is not a Client as of the date of this Agreement (a "New Client"), SHPS shall provide such Care Management Services to the New Client in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that HPS (or one of its affiliates other than SHPS) enters into an agreement to provide Health Assistance with a client which is not a Client as of the date of this Agreement (a "New Client"), SHPS shall provide such Care Management Services to the New Client in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The contract now specifies that HPS enters agreements for 'Health Assistance' (previously modified) but SHPS still provides 'Care Management Services'. If 'Health Assistance' and 'Care Management Services' are not identical, this creates a contradiction about what services SHPS must provide to the New Client. Health Assistance was defined as limited support in a previous modification, so the services SHPS provides do not align with what HPS provides.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that HPS (or one of its affiliates other than SHPS) enters into an agreement to provide Health Assistance with a client which is not a Client as of the date of this Agreement (a "New Client"), SHPS shall provide such Care Management Services to the New Client in accordance with the terms of this Agreement. HPS will pay to SHPS all Care Management Revenues collected from such New Client, and SHPS will pay a commission to HPS equal to five percent (5%) of such amount received by SHPS from HPS pursuant to this Section 2.4. HPS shall pay such amount to SHPS within fifteen days following the end of each month. At the time of payment HPS shall submit to SHPS a schedule for the month of payment setting forth the calculation of fees payable under this Section 2.4 and Care 2 3 Management Revenues by New Client. For convenience, the parties acknowledge that HPS will deduct amounts owed by SHPS under this Section 2.4 from the amounts owed by HPS under this Section 2.4 and reflect such deductions in the schedule prepared by HPS.
perturbation.justification: The changed text specifies that HPS enters agreements for 'Health Assistance' (previously defined as limited support) while SHPS still provides 'Care Management Services'. The contradicted text reiterates this discrepancy. Since 'Health Assistance' and 'Care Management Services' are distinct, this creates a contradiction regarding the services SHPS must provide to the New Client. The change introduces misaligned terminology, where the services HPS is contracted for don't match the services SHPS is obligated to provide, leading to an in-text contradiction as defined.
